headline: Intuit (NASDAQ:INTU): A GARP Opportunity with Strong Growth and Low Valuation
summary: Intuit (INTU) combines strong earnings growth with a low valuation, making it a compelling GARP stock. Its PEG ratio and below-industry P/E suggest affordable growth potential.

headline: Intuit (INTU) Is Down 8.8% After Leveraging New Debt Amid AI Shifts And Cost Cuts – Has The Bull Case Changed?
summary: In early June 2026, Intuit completed two fixed-rate senior unsecured note offerings totaling about US$1.75 billion, including 4.950% notes due 2031 and 5.500% notes due 2036, while continuing to showcase its Mailchimp Analytics AI product and investor outreach at major conferences. These funding and product moves come as Intuit confronts AI-driven competitive pressures, pricing scrutiny around TurboTax, and a large workforce reduction aimed at boosting efficiency and reinforcing its...

headline: MSCI’s New AI-Focused CTO and Silicon Valley Hub Could Be A Game Changer For MSCI (MSCI)
summary: In early June 2026, MSCI Inc. appointed former Intuit technology leader Kashi Kakarla as Chief Technology Officer and Head of Product Engineering, and confirmed plans to open a new Silicon Valley office to advance its AI and technology agenda. This move signals MSCI’s intent to embed AI more deeply into its indices, analytics and private markets offerings, potentially reshaping how investment professionals access and use its data and tools. We’ll now examine how Kakarla’s AI-focused remit...
